input.headline: In a small Alaska town, Canada-U.S. tensions are upending a longstanding relationship

raw.headline: In a small Alaska town, Canada-U.S. tensions are upending a longstanding relationship

winner: The mayor of the tiny Alaskan town announced that due to rising tensions with Canada, the local bakery will no longer offer maple syrup on its pancakes — they're switching to molasses, just to be safe.

candidates.text: Why did the Canadian geese refuse to visit the Alaskan town? They heard the border was *fowl* play!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'fowl' and 'follow' are clever and specific to the headline's context, with concise and surprising humor."}

candidates.text: Now that the border dispute is heating up, the townspeople of Nome are considering renaming their local coffee shop "The Cold Warmer.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'Cold War' and 'warmer' add surprise and specificity, tying the joke directly to the headline's geopolitical context with a touch of wordplay."}

candidates.text: The border agents finally caught him smuggling maple syrup into Alaska—turns out he was just trying to add *American* flavor to his Canadian breakfast.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and has a mild twist, but the humor feels forced and the connection to the headline about tensions is weak."}

candidates.text: The mayor of the tiny Alaskan town announced that due to rising tensions with Canada, the local bakery will no longer offer maple syrup on its pancakes — they're switching to molasses, just to be safe.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses the headline's context in a surprising and humorous way by linking international tensions to a local bakery change."}

candidates.text: The town's long-standing friendship finally collapsed today when the locals realized they were both using the same shovel to bury the same secret.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the shovel and secret metaphor, but it lacks specificity to the Canada-U.S. context and feels a bit forced in relation to the headline."}

candidates.text: The local friendship was ruined because the neighbors couldn't agree on whether they were having a border dispute or just a chilly reception.
The local friendship collapsed when a neighbor tried to move the property line three inches to the west, forcing both parties to deploy tactical flashbangs over the shared bird feeder.
Why did the Canadian moose cross the border? To prove it wasn't *deer*ing with America!
The two countries are getting so tense, even the moose are debating whether to cross the border or just take a long walk on the Canadian side.
